title: satureja alpina var. granatensis
intro: Satureja alpina var. granatensis, also known as the Spanish savory, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as carvacrol and thymol. The Spanish savory can be consumed in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which may provide health benefits like reducing stress, improving digestion, and supporting immune function.
